steps.title: Use the quantity clue
steps.content: The passage contrasts Mendel's team finding over one hundred satellites with many astronomers finding only one or two. The blank needs a word meaning numerous.

steps.title: Eliminate mismatched meanings
steps.content:
• Choice A describes lower quality, not a large number.
  • Choice B means lacking pattern, not numerous.
  • Choice D means probable, but the discoveries have already happened.

steps.title: Choose the quantity word
steps.content: Plentiful means existing in large amounts, so it matches the evidence that the team has spotted over one hundred satellites. The answer is C.
